title: Simulacra and Simian
description: It is a pleasure—one of life’s greatest—to take something stupid deadly seriously.
hook: Taking stupid things deadly seriously is a pleasure—and this essay on simulacra proves it can yield real insight.

title: Jeff Mills Loves to Forget
description: "How techno’s most vaunted architect is still building sonic futures."
hook: Jeff Mills built techno's future while refusing to look back. A portrait of an artist addicted to sonic forgetting.

title: The World Is an Easier Place Without You In It
hook: A mother-daughter story told entirely through documents—what emerges between the lines is devastating.

title: The family in the Blue Heron trailer needs to talk about Jeremy
description: The directorial debut from Sophy Romvari follows a family in crisis from the perspective of an 8-year-old girl.
hook: A family implodes around one unspeakable kid, seen through an 8-year-old girl's bewildered eyes. Debut that cuts.

title: Tokyo Adrift
description:
Joshua Hunt on Ukrainian sumo, the Japanese far right, and the changing face of the country

The post Tokyo Adrift appeared first on Harper's Magazine.
hook: Ukrainian sumo wrestlers, Japan's far right, and one country's identity crisis played out on the ring.
